tags.name: Assistants
tags.description: An assistant is a configured instance of a graph.

tags.name: Threads
tags.description: A thread contains the accumulated outputs of a group of runs.

tags.name: Thread Runs
tags.description: A run is an invocation of a graph / assistant on a thread. It updates the state of the thread.

tags.name: Stateless Runs
tags.description: A run is an invocation of a graph / assistant, with no state or memory persistence.

tags.name: Crons
tags.description: A cron is a periodic run that recurs on a given schedule. The repeats can be isolated, or share state in a thread

tags.name: Store
tags.description: Store is an API for managing persistent key-value store (long-term memory) that is available from any thread.

tags.name: A2A
tags.description: Agent-to-Agent Protocol related endpoints for exposing assistants as A2A-compliant agents.

tags.name: MCP
tags.description: Model Context Protocol related endpoints for exposing an agent as an MCP server.

tags.name: System
tags.description: System endpoints for health checks, metrics, and server information.

tags.name: Streaming
tags.description: Thread-centric streaming endpoints. Provides a structured command/event surface over SSE+HTTP; WebSocket is also supported at `/threads/{thread_id}/stream/events` (not documented here — OpenAPI 3.1 does not describe WebSocket).

post.description:
Create an assistant.

An initial version of the assistant will be created and the assistant is set to that version. To change versions, use the `POST /assistants/{assistant_id}/latest` endpoint.

Search for assistants.

This endpoint also functions as the endpoint to list all assistants.

schema.title: Response Search Assistants Assistants Search Post

post.description: Get the count of assistants matching the specified criteria.

get.description: Get an assistant by ID.

delete.description:
Delete an assistant by ID.

All versions of the assistant will be deleted as well.

parameters.description: If true, delete all threads with metadata.assistant_id matching this assistant, along with runs and checkpoints belonging to those threads. Auth filters are applied, so threads not visible to the user will not be deleted.
parameters.name: delete_threads

get.description: This endpoint streams output in real-time from a thread. The stream will include the output of each run executed sequentially on the thread and will remain open indefinitely. It is the responsibility of the calling client to close the connection.

schema.title: Last Event ID
schema.description: The ID of the last event received. Used to resume streaming from a specific point. Pass '-' to resume from the beginning.

schema.title: Stream Modes
schema.description: Stream modes to control which events are returned. 'lifecycle' returns only run start/end events, 'run_modes' returns all run events (default behavior), 'state_update' returns only state update events.

The server will send a stream of events in SSE format.

**Example event**:

id: 1

event: message

data: {}

post.description:
Open a connection-scoped SSE event stream for a thread. The request body is a `ProtocolEventStreamRequest` carrying channel and namespace filters; the server replies with `Content-Type: text/event-stream` and pushes matching `ProtocolEvent` frames for the lifetime of the connection. Closing the connection unsubscribes — no state is persisted server-side.

Reconnect: clients pass the last `seq` they received as `since` in the body. Buffered events with `seq > since` are replayed before the stream goes live. The endpoint is POST-only, so browser-native `EventSource` auto-resume (`Last-Event-ID`) does not apply — clients drive resume explicitly via the body.

schema.title: Thread Id
schema.description: The ID of the thread to observe.

Stream of `ProtocolEvent` frames. Each SSE frame uses the event's `method` as the `event:` field and a JSON-encoded `ProtocolEvent` as the `data:` field; `id:` is set to the event's `seq`.

**Example frame:**

id: 42

event: messages

data: {"type":"event","event_id":"42","seq":42,"method":"messages","params":{"namespace":[],"timestamp":1700000000000,"data":{...}}}

get.description: Join a run stream. This endpoint streams output in real-time from a run similar to the /threads/__THREAD_ID__/runs/stream endpoint. If the run has been created with `stream_resumable=true`, the stream can be resumed from the last seen event ID.

schema.title: Stream Mode
schema.description: The mode to stream the run in. If not provided, the default mode will be used.

schema.title: Cancel On Disconnect
schema.description: If true, the run will be cancelled if the client disconnects.

The server will send a stream of events in SSE format.

**Example event**:

id: 1

event: message

data: {}

parameters.description: Action to take when cancelling the run. Possible values are `interrupt` or `rollback`. `interrupt` will simply cancel the run. `rollback` will cancel the run and delete the run and associated checkpoints afterwards.

post.description:
Communicate with an assistant using the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Protocol over JSON-RPC 2.0.
This endpoint accepts a JSON-RPC envelope and dispatches based on `method`.

**Supported Methods:**
- `message/send`: Send a message and wait for the final Task result.
- `message/stream`: Send a message and receive Server-Sent Events (SSE) JSON-RPC responses.
- `tasks/get`: Fetch the current state of a Task by ID.
- `tasks/cancel`: Request cancellation (currently not supported; returns an error).

**LangGraph Mapping:**
- `message.contextId` maps to LangGraph `thread_id`.

**Notes:**
- Only `text` and `data` parts are supported; `file` parts are not.
- If `message.contextId` is omitted, a new context is created.
- Text parts require the assistant input schema to include a `messages` field.

parameters.name: assistant_id
parameters.description: The ID of the assistant to communicate with

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: For `message/stream`, must include `text/event-stream`. For all other methods, use `application/json`.

params.description: Method parameters; shape depends on the method.

role.description: A2A role for the message sender.

data.description: Structured JSON merged into the assistant input.

contextId.description: Conversation context ID; maps to LangGraph thread_id.

taskId.description: Optional task ID. Currently ignored by this implementation.

historyLength.description: Maximum number of history messages to include.

message_stream_with_context.summary: Stream multi-turn messages using the same contextId
message_stream_with_context.description: Send a streaming message; reuse the same contextId for subsequent turns to keep conversation state.

interrupt_resume_text.summary: Resume an interrupted task with a text reply
interrupt_resume_text.description: When a task is in input-required state, send a normal text part with the same contextId and taskId to resume.

parts.text: Here is the info you asked for.

interrupt_resume_data_parallel.summary: Resume parallel interrupts with a data part
interrupt_resume_data_parallel.description: Advanced: if your agent supports parallel interrupts, provide a structured resume payload in a data part with the same contextId and taskId.

200.description: JSON-RPC response for non-streaming methods. For `message/stream`, the response is an SSE stream of JSON-RPC envelopes.

result.description: Success result containing task information or task details

error.message: Invalid request: Missing required parameter: contextId

schema.description: SSE stream of JSON-RPC response objects.

400.description: Bad Request (invalid JSON-RPC, invalid params, or missing Accept header).

post.description:
Implemented according to the Streamable HTTP Transport specification.
Sends a JSON-RPC 2.0 message to the server.

- **Request**: Provide an object with `jsonrpc`, `id`, `method`, and optional `params`.
- **Response**: Returns a JSON-RPC response or acknowledgment.

**Notes:**
- Stateless: Sessions are not persisted across requests.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Accept header must include both 'application/json' and 'text/event-stream' media types.

schema.description: A JSON-RPC 2.0 request, notification, or response object.

202.description: Notification or response accepted; no content body.

400.description: Bad request: invalid JSON or message format, or unacceptable Accept header.

500.description: Internal server error or unexpected failure.

get.description: Implemented according to the Streamable HTTP Transport specification.

405.description: GET method not allowed; streaming not supported.

delete.description:
Implemented according to the Streamable HTTP Transport specification.
Terminate an MCP session. The server implementation is stateless, so this is a no-op.

post.description:
Send a single protocol command scoped to a thread. The request body is a `ProtocolCommand` envelope with a `method` (e.g. `run.start`, `input.respond`, `agent.getTree`) and method-specific `params`. The response is either a `ProtocolSuccess` (with method-specific `result`) or a `ProtocolError`.

Commands that create runs (`run.start`, `input.respond`) leave the run executing in the background on the worker queue. Event streaming for that run is observed via a concurrent `POST /threads/{thread_id}/stream/events` connection.

WebSocket clients use the same command envelope in-band on `/threads/{thread_id}/stream/events` and additionally have access to `subscription.subscribe` / `subscription.unsubscribe` over the same connection.

schema.title: Thread Id
schema.description: The ID of the thread the command applies to.

feedback_keys.description:
Generate pre-signed URLs for assigning feedback keys to the run.

For each key specified, a unique URL will be generated that can be called to assign the feedback key to the run. See [LangSmith Feedback](https://docs.langchain.com/langsmith/observability-concepts#feedback) for more details.

graph_id.description: The ID of the graph to filter by. The graph ID is normally set in your langgraph.json configuration.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the assistant to filter by. The filtering logic will match (case insensitive) assistants where 'name' is a substring of the assistant name.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Metadata to filter versions by. Exact match filter for each KV pair.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: The maximum number of versions to return.

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: The number of versions to skip.

ids.title: Ids
ids.description: List of thread IDs to include. Others are excluded.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Thread metadata to filter on.

select.title: Select
select.description: Specify which fields to return. If not provided, all fields are returned.

extract.title: Extract
extract.description: Map of alias to JSONB path for extracting specific values. Paths must start with values, metadata, config, or interrupts. Example: {"last_msg": "values.messages[-1]"}.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Thread metadata to filter on.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: The time the thread was created.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: The last time the thread was updated.

state_updated_at.title: State Updated At
state_updated_at.description: The last time the thread state was updated.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: The thread metadata.

values.title: Values
values.description: The current state of the thread.

interrupts.title: Interrupts
interrupts.description: The current interrupts of the thread.

ttl.title: TTL Info
ttl.description: TTL information if set for this thread. Only present when ?include=ttl is passed.

extracted.title: Extracted
extracted.description: Extracted values from thread JSONB columns, populated when extract is specified in search request.

thread_id.title: Thread Id
thread_id.description: The ID of the thread. If not provided, a random UUID will be generated.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Metadata to add to thread.

if_exists.title: If Exists
if_exists.description: How to handle duplicate creation. Must be either 'raise' (raise error if duplicate), or 'do_nothing' (return existing thread).

strategy.description: The TTL strategy. 'delete' removes the entire thread. 'keep_latest' prunes old checkpoints but keeps the thread and its latest state.

ttl.description: The time-to-live in minutes from now until thread should be swept.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Metadata to merge with existing thread metadata.

strategy.description: The TTL strategy. 'delete' removes the entire thread. 'keep_latest' prunes old checkpoints but keeps the thread and its latest state.

ttl.description: The time-to-live in minutes from now until thread should be swept.

thread_ids.title: Thread IDs
thread_ids.description: List of thread IDs to prune.

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.description: The prune strategy. 'delete' removes threads entirely. 'keep_latest' prunes old checkpoints but keeps threads and their latest state.

checkpoint.title: Checkpoint
checkpoint.description: The checkpoint to get the state for.

ThreadStateCheckpointRequest.title: ThreadStateCheckpointRequest
ThreadStateCheckpointRequest.description: Payload for getting the state of a thread at a checkpoint.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: The maximum number of states to return.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Filter states by metadata key-value pairs.

values.title: Values
values.description: The values to update the state with.

checkpoint.title: Checkpoint
checkpoint.description: The checkpoint to update the state of.

as_node.title: As Node
as_node.description: Update the state as if this node had just executed.

ThreadStateUpdate.title: ThreadStateUpdate
ThreadStateUpdate.description: Payload for updating the state of a thread.

command.description: The command associated with the update.

as_node.description: Update the state as if this node had just executed.

ThreadStateUpdateResponse.title: ThreadStateUpdateResponse
ThreadStateUpdateResponse.description: Response for adding state to a thread.

thread_id.description: Unique identifier for the thread associated with this checkpoint.

checkpoint_ns.description: Namespace for the checkpoint, used for organization and retrieval.

checkpoint_id.description: Optional unique identifier for the checkpoint itself.

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: A list of strings representing the namespace path.

key.title: Key
key.description: The unique identifier for the item within the namespace.

value.title: Value
value.description: A dictionary containing the item's data.

index.title: Index
index.description: Controls search indexing - null (use defaults), false (disable), or list of field paths to index.

ttl.title: TTL
ttl.description: Optional time-to-live in minutes for the item, or null for no expiration.

StorePutRequest.title: StorePutRequest
StorePutRequest.description: Request to store or update an item.

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: A list of strings representing the namespace path.

key.title: Key
key.description: The unique identifier for the item.

namespace_prefix.title: Namespace Prefix
namespace_prefix.description: List of strings representing the namespace prefix.

filter.title: Filter
filter.description: Optional dictionary of key-value pairs to filter results.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of items to return (default is 10).

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: Number of items to skip before returning results (default is 0).

query.title: Query
query.description: Optional natural language search query. If not provided, items are returned ordered by last updated time (listing mode). If provided, performs a natural language search over item contents.

refresh_ttl.title: Refresh TTL
refresh_ttl.description: Whether to refresh the TTL on items returned by this search. If null, uses the store's default behavior.

StoreSearchRequest.title: StoreSearchRequest
StoreSearchRequest.description: Request to list or search for items within a namespace prefix. Without a query, lists items by last updated time. With a query, performs natural language search.

prefix.title: Prefix
prefix.description: Optional list of strings representing the prefix to filter namespaces.

suffix.title: Suffix
suffix.description: Optional list of strings representing the suffix to filter namespaces.

max_depth.title: Max Depth
max_depth.description: Optional integer specifying the maximum depth of namespaces to return.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of namespaces to return (default is 100).

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: Number of namespaces to skip before returning results (default is 0).

namespace.description: The namespace of the item. A namespace is analogous to a document's directory.

key.description: The unique identifier of the item within its namespace. In general, keys needn't be globally unique.

value.description: The value stored in the item. This is the document itself.

created_at.description: The timestamp when the item was created.

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the item was last updated.

Item.description: Represents a single document or data entry in the graph's Store. Items are used to store cross-thread memories.

status.title: Status
status.description: Filter runs by status to cancel. Must be one of 'pending', 'running', or 'all'.

thread_id.title: Thread Id
thread_id.description: The ID of the thread containing runs to cancel.

run_ids.title: Run Ids
run_ids.description: List of run IDs to cancel.

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description: Error response returned from the server

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.description: The cron schedule to execute this job on.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: IANA timezone for the cron schedule (e.g. 'America/New_York'). Defaults to null, which is treated as UTC.

end_time.title: End Time
end_time.description: The end date to stop running the cron.

assistant_id.description: The assistant ID or graph name to run. If using graph name, will default to the assistant automatically created from that graph by the server.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Metadata to assign to the cron job runs.

context.title: Context
context.description: Static context added to the assistant.

webhook.title: Webhook
webhook.description: Webhook to call after LangGraph API call is done.

interrupt_before.title: Interrupt Before
interrupt_before.description: Nodes to interrupt immediately before they get executed.

interrupt_after.title: Interrupt After
interrupt_after.description: Nodes to interrupt immediately after they get executed.

multitask_strategy.title: Multitask Strategy
multitask_strategy.description: Multitask strategy to use. Must be one of 'reject', 'interrupt', 'rollback', or 'enqueue'.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether the cron job should be enabled. Disabled crons are not executed.

stream_subgraphs.title: Stream Subgraphs
stream_subgraphs.description: Whether to stream output from subgraphs.

stream_resumable.title: Stream Resumable
stream_resumable.description: Whether to persist the stream chunks in order to resume the stream later.

durability.title: Durability
durability.description: Durability level for the run. Must be one of `sync`, `async`, or `exit`. See [durability modes](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/durable-execution#durability-modes) for more details.

ThreadCronCreate.title: ThreadCronCreate
ThreadCronCreate.description: Payload for creating a thread-specific cron job (runs on the same thread).

on_disconnect.title: On Disconnect
on_disconnect.description: The disconnect mode to use. Must be one of 'cancel' or 'continue'.

on_disconnect.title: On Disconnect
on_disconnect.description: The disconnect mode to use. Must be one of 'cancel' or 'continue'.

ProtocolChannel.title: Protocol Channel
ProtocolChannel.description: Subscribable event channel. One of the eight fixed channels or a user-defined `custom:<name>` namespaced custom channel emitted by a stream transformer. `debug` and `checkpoints` were removed from the protocol in `@langchain/protocol@0.0.10` — task-level debug info now flows on the `tasks` channel, and checkpoint pointers ride on `values` events as an attached `checkpoint` envelope.

ProtocolNamespace.title: Protocol Namespace
ProtocolNamespace.description: Hierarchical path identifying a position in the agent tree. Empty array denotes the root agent; each segment names a nested subgraph.

ProtocolEventStreamRequest.title: ProtocolEventStreamRequest
ProtocolEventStreamRequest.description: Body of `POST /threads/{thread_id}/stream/events`. Filters the connection-scoped SSE stream.

channels.description: Channels to subscribe to for the lifetime of this connection.

namespaces.description: Prefix-match filter. An event matches if its namespace starts with any of these prefixes. Omit for all namespaces.

depth.description: Max depth below the namespace prefix. Omit for unbounded depth.

since.description: Replay events with `seq` greater than this value before streaming live events.

ProtocolCommand.title: ProtocolCommand
ProtocolCommand.description: A client → server command envelope. Known methods: `run.start`, `input.respond`, `subscription.subscribe` (WS only), `subscription.unsubscribe` (WS only), `agent.getTree`.

id.description: Client-assigned command id; echoed in the response.

params.description: Method-specific parameters. See the CDDL spec for per-method shapes.

ProtocolResponseMeta.title: ProtocolResponseMeta
ProtocolResponseMeta.description: Optional metadata attached to command responses.

applied_through_seq.description: Highest event `seq` observed on the session when this response was generated.

id.description: The `id` of the offending command, or null if the envelope was unparseable.

ProtocolEvent.title: ProtocolEvent
ProtocolEvent.description: A server → client event. Sent as JSON over WebSocket, or as the `data:` field of an SSE frame (with `event:` set to `method` and `id:` set to `seq`).

event_id.description: Unique ID for reconnection (mirrored to SSE `id:`).

seq.description: Monotonic per-session sequence number for ordering and replay.

timestamp.description: Wall-clock milliseconds since Unix epoch. Use `seq` for ordering.

data.description: Channel-specific payload. See the CDDL spec for per-channel shapes.

node.description: Graph node that produced this event (messages, tools).
